channels.title: Physical World
channels.blurb: QR on paper, objects, stickers, skin, stone — the physical thing runs live code inside whoever looks at it.

channels.title: Verify-Before-Exec
channels.blurb: Tamper-evident, un-killable, trust-the-hash-not-the-host. Run untrusted-CDN code and prove it wasn't changed.

channels.title: Agent to Agent
channels.blurb: MCP tools, brainstems as bytecode, signed dead-drops — machines using the stack with no human and no browser.

channels.title: Human & Personal
channels.blurb: Twins, gifts, memorials, time capsules, dead-man switches — the emotional edge of a serverless, forever stack.

channels.title: Self-Building
channels.blurb: The loop, the tumbler, systems that improve and verify themselves — including this library.

channels.title: Wildcard
channels.blurb: The strange stacks that shouldn't work but provably do. Go weird, stay real.

channels.title: Play
channels.blurb: Games and toys — playable, no server: scan-to-join, pass-and-play, physics sandboxes, real-time fun.

channels.title: Art
channels.blurb: Generative and camera visuals — beauty seeded by a hash, live data, or your own face.

channels.title: Sound
channels.blurb: Instruments and sonification — the Web Audio API turned into synths, toys, and data you can hear.

channels.title: Social
channels.blurb: Collaborative, no backend — shared canvases, tanks, walls and stories built on GitHub Issues.
